name: Polytetrafluoroethylene (PTFE) Laser Cleaning Dataset
description: Comprehensive laser cleaning parameters and material properties for Polytetrafluoroethylene (PTFE). Includes thermal, optical, mechanical, and laser interaction properties validated against industry standards.

license.name: Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International
license.description: Free to share and adapt with attribution

measurementTechnique: Laser ablation testing, material characterization, spectroscopy

includedInDataCatalog.name: Z-Beam Material Properties Database
includedInDataCatalog.description: Comprehensive laser cleaning parameters and material properties for industrial applications

powerRange.description: Optimal average power range for Polytetrafluoroethylene surface cleaning without thermal degradation

wavelength.description: Near-infrared wavelength optimized for Polytetrafluoroethylene absorption characteristics and minimal subsurface damage

spotSize.description: Beam spot diameter for precise Polytetrafluoroethylene surface cleaning resolution

repetitionRate.description: Optimal repetition rate for efficient Polytetrafluoroethylene cleaning with thermal management

energyDensity.description: Fluence threshold for effective Polytetrafluoroethylene contaminant removal without substrate damage

pulseWidth.description: Nanosecond pulse duration for controlled ablation of Polytetrafluoroethylene surface contaminants

scanSpeed.description: Optimal scanning speed for uniform Polytetrafluoroethylene surface treatment

passCount.description: Recommended number of passes for complete Polytetrafluoroethylene surface cleaning

overlapRatio.description: Optimal beam overlap for uniform Polytetrafluoroethylene surface coverage

power.description: Typical range for 1064 nm pulsed lasers in polymer cleaning to avoid thermal degradation of PTFE (low thermal conductivity ~0.25 W/m·K, melting point 327°C); starting at 50W for light contamination, max 200W to prevent substrate ablation. Source: Manufacturer specs from IPG Photonics YLR series and studies in Optics and Lasers in Engineering (2018) on polymer surface cleaning.

scan_speed.description: Effective speeds balance energy delivery and heat dissipation; 500 mm/s for heavy contamination (e.g., oils), up to 3000 mm/s for light dust; recommended 1500 mm/s for optimal PTFE cleaning without residue. Source: Industry standards from Laserax equipment manuals and Journal of Cleaner Production (2020) case studies on industrial polymer prep.

pulse_width.description: Nanosecond regime preferred for cost-effective cleaning of PTFE; picosecond (ps) or femtosecond reduces heat-affected zone (HAZ <10 μm) but increases cost; 20 ns minimizes thermal damage due to PTFE's poor conductivity. Avoid fs for industrial scale. Source: Laser physics research in Applied Surface Science (2019) on polymer ablation thresholds.

repetition_rate.description: Typical for 1064 nm cleaning to achieve fluence without excessive heat buildup; 30 kHz recommended for PTFE to balance speed and avoid accumulation (high freq risks melting); lower for heavy soiling. Trade-off: higher freq speeds process but increases HAZ. Source: Academic literature in Lasers in Manufacturing and Materials Processing (2021).

energy_density.description: Fluence range for contaminant removal without exceeding PTFE ablation threshold (~1.5-2 J/cm² at 1064 nm); recommended 1.0 J/cm² for safe cleaning of surfaces like rust or oils, considering PTFE's high reflectivity (~90% at 1064 nm). Source: Ablation threshold studies in Journal of Applied Physics (2017) and Optics Express (2022) on fluoropolymers.

spot_size.description: Beam diameter for precise control; 100 μm for fine details (e.g., mold prep), 500 μm for large areas; 200 μm recommended for uniform PTFE cleaning to maintain fluence. Source: Equipment specifications from Coherent Armonia series and industry reports in Surface and Coatings Technology (2019).

pass_count.description: Single pass for light contamination, multi-pass for layered residues; 2 passes recommended for complete PTFE surface prep without damage, allowing cooling between passes. Source: Industry practice in case studies from Laser Cleaning International and Materials Science and Engineering: A (2020).

overlap_ratio.description: Ensures uniform coverage without gaps or overexposure; 50% recommended for PTFE to achieve even cleaning while minimizing heat input, adjustable to 30% for speed or 80% for heavy soiling. Source: Process optimization studies in International Journal of Advanced Manufacturing Technology (2018).
